# Beaconing-destination detection by per (host,dst) upload-to-avg-download ratio (T1048)

**Dataset**: `gx_07_flows`

Surfaces individual exfil destinations rather than just noisy hosts by grouping on the `(host, dst)` pair: a destination that receives large totals out while returning only small average responses behaves like a drop site or beaconing C2. It sums egress and averages ingress per pair, then `round(divide(to_float(out_to_dst), to_float(avg_in)))` gives an upload-to-response ratio; pairs above the threshold are the suspicious channels. Grouping on two keys is what distinguishes per-destination triage from a per-host rollup that would hide which endpoint the data went to.

```sql
dataset = gx_07_flows
| comp sum(bytes_out) as out_to_dst, avg(bytes_in) as avg_in by host, dst
| filter avg_in > 0
| alter ratio = round(divide(to_float(out_to_dst), to_float(avg_in)))
| filter ratio > 100
| sort desc out_to_dst
| fields host, dst, out_to_dst, avg_in, ratio
```
